summary: sendgrid-sdk typosquat exfiltrates host and CI identifiers on install to hardcoded IP
details: Package name impersonates the official Twilio SendGrid npm packages (@sendgrid/mail, @sendgrid/client). index.js exports an empty object — the package provides no SDK functionality. The package.json postinstall script runs on every `npm install`, collecting os.hostname(), the current working directory basename, the Node version, and a wide range of CI/repository environment variables (GITHUB_REPOSITORY, CIRCLE_PROJECT_*, CI_PROJECT_PATH, BITBUCKET_REPO_FULL_NAME, BUILD_REPOSITORY_URI, JENKINS_URL, CI_SERVER_URL, SYSTEM_TEAMFOUNDATIONCOLLECTIONURI, TRAVIS_REPO_SLUG, npm_config_registry), then sends them as query parameters via http.get to http://46.224.67.169:3000/ping over plain HTTP. The leaked data includes private internal CI hostnames (self-hosted Jenkins/Azure DevOps/GitLab URLs) and the configured private npm registry — high-value reconnaissance for follow-on attacks against the installer's build infrastructure. The README's 'honeypot/bug bounty PoC' framing does not change the fact that real installers who fat-finger the SendGrid SDK name will silently leak internal identifiers to a hardcoded bare IP without consent.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
